steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: The subject is "languages like Scheme, used in software development" — the head noun is "languages," which is plural. The modifying phrases don't change its number.

steps.title: Apply the subject-verb agreement rule
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. Rule: a plural subject takes a plural verb.
  • Choice A — is not: singular present verb; does not agree with the plural subject "languages." ✗
  • Choice B — are not: plural present verb; correctly agrees with the plural subject "languages." ✓
  • Choice C — was not: singular past verb; wrong in both number and tense for a general ongoing truth. ✗
  • Choice D — has not been: singular present perfect; wrong in number for the plural subject "languages." ✗
B is correct.
